description: TDD: Write failing tests for full audio processing Lambda logic (S3 download, Polly synthesis, S3 upload, DynamoDB update) and then implement the Lambda handler. Update CDK stack to grant S3 read/write permissions and add environment variables for bucket names. Update the Polly task to use dynamic parameters from the Lambda output.
steps: Step 1 - Write failing tests in tests/unit/test_audio_processing.py (new file) that verify: (a) Lambda downloads from input S3 bucket when input is audio file, (b) Lambda calls Polly synthesize_speech when input is a text prompt (.txt file with text content), (c) Lambda uploads processed file to output S3 bucket with naming convention (processed/{audio_id}_{timestamp}.mp3), (d) Lambda updates DynamoDB with output location, file size, and COMPLETED status, (e) Lambda returns structured success response including outputKey, outputBucket, duration, fileSize for Step Functions, (f) Error handling: graceful failure when S3 download fails, when Polly fails, when upload fails. Use unittest.mock to mock boto3 clients (s3, polly, dynamodb).

Step 2 - Implement the full audio processing logic in lambda/sleep_audio_processor/handler.py: (a) Initialize boto3 clients for S3, Polly, DynamoDB using env vars INPUT_BUCKET, OUTPUT_BUCKET, TABLE_NAME, (b) Download input file from S3 input bucket to /tmp, (c) Determine processing mode: if audio file (.mp3/.wav/.ogg/.flac) -> normalize/passthrough; if text prompt -> use Polly to synthesize speech, (d) Upload processed audio to output S3 bucket with key: processed/{audio_id}_{uuid}.mp3, (e) Update DynamoDB record with outputBucket, outputKey, fileSize, status=COMPLETED, updatedAt, (f) Return structured response with all output metadata for Step Functions downstream consumption, (g) Handle errors gracefully: catch boto3 exceptions, log structured errors, update DynamoDB status to FAILED if possible, re-raise for Step Functions error handling.

Step 3 - Update cdk_base/cdk_base_stack.py: (a) Store output_bucket as a variable (currently created but not stored), (b) Grant Lambda read access to input_bucket (input_bucket.grant_read(process_audio_fn)), (c) Grant Lambda write access to output_bucket (output_bucket.grant_write(process_audio_fn)), (d) Add INPUT_BUCKET and OUTPUT_BUCKET environment variables to the Lambda function, (e) Grant Lambda polly:SynthesizeSpeech permission, (f) Increase Lambda memory to 512MB and timeout to 60s (audio processing needs more resources), (g) Update Polly task parameters to use dynamic values from Lambda output or remove placeholder since Lambda now handles Polly directly.

Step 4 - Update CDK tests to reflect infrastructure changes: (a) Update test_cdk_base_stack.py or test_lambda_integration.py to verify new env vars (INPUT_BUCKET, OUTPUT_BUCKET), (b) Verify S3 read/write grants exist for the Lambda, (c) Verify Lambda memory and timeout settings, (d) Verify Polly permissions for the Lambda role.

Step 5 - Update the Step Functions state machine: (a) The UpdateStatusCompleted task should now include output metadata from the Lambda result (outputKey, outputBucket, fileSize), (b) Ensure the state machine properly routes the enriched Lambda output through the pipeline.
Step 6 - Run all tests (python3 -m pytest tests/ -v) and cdk synth -c environment=dev to verify everything passes.
acceptance_criteria: All new tests in test_audio_processing.py pass
All existing 148 tests continue to pass
Lambda handler downloads from S3, processes audio (or calls Polly for text), uploads to output bucket, updates DynamoDB
Lambda returns structured response with outputKey, outputBucket, fileSize, status fields
CDK stack grants Lambda S3 read on input bucket, S3 write on output bucket, polly:SynthesizeSpeech
Lambda has INPUT_BUCKET, OUTPUT_BUCKET, TABLE_NAME environment variables
cdk synth -c environment=dev succeeds without errors
Error paths are tested: S3 failures, Polly failures update DynamoDB status to FAILED
verification: unset NODE_OPTIONS && cd /projects/sandbox/cdk-sleep-py-kiro && python3 -m pytest tests/ -v
unset NODE_OPTIONS && cd /projects/sandbox/cdk-sleep-py-kiro && npx cdk synth -c environment=dev --quiet
